sections.summary: This standard applies to unrefined shea butter intended for direct consumption or as an ingredient in food products.

Defines shea butter as vegetable fat from kernels of Vitellaria paradoxa and unrefined shea butter as oleaginous material obtained from those kernels by manual or mechanical (thermal or cold-press) methods possibly purified by washing, settling, filtering and centrifuging, and also defines 'lot' as a specified quantity with uniform characteristics.

Product definitions: shea butter is vegetable fat from Vitellaria paradoxa kernels, and unrefined shea butter is the oleaginous material obtained by manual or mechanical (thermal or cold-press) processes and may be purified by washing, settling, filtering and centrifuging.
'Lot' is defined as a specified quantity of unrefined shea butter that has uniform characteristics enabling the quantity to be assessed.

Specifies essential composition and quality factors for unrefined shea butter, including hygienic storage/handling of raw materials, prohibition on mixing with other fats and freedom from foreign matter, organoleptic requirements (colour, aroma, flavour, absence of rancidity), grade-specific quality limits for water, free fatty acids, peroxide value and insoluble impurities (Table 1) with intended uses for Grade I and II, chemical/physical parameter ranges (Table 2), and fatty acid composition ranges (Table 3).
Requires raw materials to be stored and handled under hygienic conditions while maintaining their physicochemical and microbiological characteristics.

Declares that unrefined shea butter must not be mixed with other fats, must be free of foreign matter, and sets organoleptic and grade-based quality criteria (see Table 1) including intended uses for Grade I (direct consumption) and Grade II (food industry).
Requires unrefined shea butter to have the characteristic colour (ivory to yellowish), aroma and flavour of the product and to be free from any rancidity.

Mandates compliance with Table 1 quality criteria for unrefined shea butter, specifying grade-specific maximum/minimum limits for water content, free fatty acids, peroxide value and insoluble impurities, and states the permitted uses for Grade I and Grade II products.

Provides chemical and physical parameter ranges for unrefined shea butter (Table 2), including relative density (20 °C), density (40 °C), saponification value, iodine value, unsaponifiable matter, refractive index (44 °C) and melting point (35–40 °C).

Specifies compliant ranges for fatty acid composition of unrefined shea butter (Table 3) expressed as percentages of total fatty acids for specific acids such as palmitic, stearic, oleic and linoleic acids and limits for minor fatty acids.
States that no food additives are permitted in unrefined shea butter.
Requires the product to comply with the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.
Recommends preparation and handling of unrefined shea butter in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex hygienic practice codes.

Requires labelling in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and that each container bear name and grade (consistent with Table 1), manufacturer name/address or trademark, country of origin, net weight, date of manufacture, shelf life, lot number/code and storage instructions, and notes that non‑retail container labelling should follow CXS 346-2021.

Mandates that product labelling comply with CXS 1-1985 and that each container display the product name and grade, manufacturer/trademark, country of origin, net weight, manufacture date, shelf life, lot number/code and storage instructions.
Advises that labelling of non-retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Container of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

Sets sampling to ISO 5555 and lists required analytical tests and corresponding standard methods (e.g., moisture, free fatty acids, density, saponification, iodine, peroxide, unsaponifiable matter, insoluble impurities, melting point, and heavy metals) to verify compliance with the quality standard.
Specifies that sampling shall be performed in accordance with ISO 5555:2001 for animal and vegetable fats and oils.

Prescribes analytical test methods for compliance verification, matching each test parameter (moisture, free fatty acids, density, saponification, iodine, peroxide, unsaponifiable matter, insoluble impurities, melting point, and lead/arsenic/iron) to specific ISO, AOAC, AOCS or IUPAC standardized procedures.
Provides bibliographic notes and Codex references cited in the standard (e.g., CXS 193-1995, CXC 1-1969, CXG 21-1997, CXS 1-1985, CXS 346-2021).
